page: Amazon Web Services g6.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz. It features 256 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, offering 22 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 1880 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute and memory performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests placing in the top 25%. The server demonstrates top-tier performance in large language model inference for small and medium models, both in prompt processing and text generation. However, for large 70B models, text generation performance drops to the bottom 25%, highlighting a hardware limitation for massive model execution. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, though large uncached read and write operations fall into the average category.

From a resource density perspective, the g6.16xlarge offers a balanced hardware profile combining dedicated x86_64 compute cores, local NVMe storage, and a mid-range enterprise GPU. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring moderate GPU acceleration without the premium cost of high-end tensor-core accelerators. It is suited for real-time LLM inference on small-to-medium models, graphics-intensive rendering, image processing, database operations, and general-purpose GPU-accelerated workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and a 3.4 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory, a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM, and 1880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the server delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier results in LLM inference for small and medium models. However, text generation for 70B models is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. This instance provides a balanced, cost-efficient hardware profile for graphics-intensive tasks, database operations, and moderate machine learning inference workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, a 22 GB NVIDIA L4 GPU, and 1880 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for moderate AI inference and graphics workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, an NVIDIA L4 GPU, and 1880 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, local NVMe storage, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU for balanced AI and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU with 64 vCPUs and 256 GB DDR4 RAM
Single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM for graphics and AI acceleration
1880 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in small and medium LLM inference tasks
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for graphics rendering, database operations, and mid-sized model inference
